videos.title: Top 10 Best Things to Do in Nicosia, Cyprus [Nicosia Travel Guide 2024]
videos.description:
Welcome to our channel where we showcase the best things to do in Nicosia the capital city of Cyprus. Join us on an exciting journey as we explore the rich cultural heritage and captivating attractions that this vibrant city has to offer.

In this video, we take you on a virtual tour of Nicosia and highlight the top activities, landmarks, and hidden gems that should be on your itinerary when visiting. Immerse yourself in the fascinating history of the city by exploring iconic landmarks such as the ancient Venetian walls, Selimiye Mosque, and Famagusta Gate.

Discover the vibrant local culture by strolling through the bustling streets of the Old Town, where you'll find traditional markets, charming cafes, and boutique shops. Indulge in the mouthwatering Cypriot cuisine at recommended local restaurants and experience the warm hospitality of the locals.

For those seeking a dose of art and history, we recommend visiting the Cyprus Museum, which houses an impressive collection of archaeological artifacts spanning centuries. Additionally, explore the Leventis Municipal Museum and its exhibitions showcasing the city's history.

Nature enthusiasts will be delighted to explore the stunning parks and gardens scattered throughout the city, such as the tranquil Municipal Gardens and the enchanting Pedieos River Linear Park. Unwind and soak in the natural beauty that Nicosia offers.

Join us in this exciting adventure as we reveal the best things to do in Nicosia, ensuring your visit to the capital becomes an unforgettable experience. Don't forget to subscribe to our channel for more travel guides and recommendations!

#nicosiatravelguide #thingstodoinnicosia #nicosiaattractions #explorenicosia #nicosiatour #nicosialandmarks #nicosiaculture #nicosiamuseums #nicosiarestaurants

▬Contents of this video▬

00:00 Introduction
00:54 Selimiye Mosque
01:37 Cyprus Museum
02:25 Ledra Street
03:15 Kyrenia
03:59 Saint Hilarion Castle
04:41 Bellapais Abbey
05:31 Famagusta
06:09 Ruins of Ancient Salamis
06:55 Buffavento Castle
07:46 Nicosia's Old Town Neighborhood

videos.transcript: [Music] nicasia is the last capital in the world to still be divided militarily in the green line at lra will Intrigue visitors who want to know what it's like to cross a city checkpoint in the 21st century on both sides of the Divide there's a lot to see like the celam my mosque a hybrid of Christian and Islamic architecture or the Cypress Museum the premier collection of ancient artifacts on the island Nicos is an un touristy business-like place but gives you a real sense of urban life in Cyprus today we share the top 10 best things to do in nicasa so you can plan a trip and experience all the exciting attractions and activities this beautiful city has to offer starting with number 10 sell my mosque here's the slightly confusing site of a mosque adapted from a former gothic church St Sophia's Cathedral the original Christian building was started during the Crusades by the Luc signant but never actually completed even though it was the foremost Church on the island Western eyes will recognize all the features of a gothic church including the Tracer windows and flying buttresses but bestriding the front of the building are two tall minettes the conversion to mosque took place after the ottoman Conquest in the 1500s when most of the Christian iconography was removed at number nine is the Cypress Museum the island is a treasure chest of ancient civilization almost every town has an excavation site close by many of the best pieces discovered at these digs are on display at the Cypress Museum the Island's biggest and best archaeological exhibition this is a gateway to Cypress Rich history and what's great about the attraction is that only pieces recovered on the island are on [Music] display at number eight ledra [Music] Street traditionally nicosia's main shopping artery Lis Street was sealed off by the militarized boundary between north and south nicasa after Bloody Christmas in 1963 the street was only reopened in 2008 and has now become a hub for Commerce once more the reopening was a big step for the improving relationship between once irreconcilable [Music] parties the shakas tower on this Lis Street has a public observation tower on its 11th floor with a wide ranging view of the city and the north south un buffers Zone as it ripples along the landscape at number seven kinia on the on the North Cypress Coast kinia is a gorgeous little Port Town framed by the formidable peaks of the pentados mountains the harbor was fortified by The venetians Who bolstered its imposing Fortress with thick rles that have survived for almost half a millennium you could take a tour of the old Town's narrow alleys and take dinner by the water to round off a perfect day trip if you visit kinia in June you can get to know the town's Turkish culture when a variety of Turkish and international bands put on concerts at number six in our list St Harian [Music] Castle high above the road that snakes down to corinia is this astounding building that deserves an hour of your time from a distance this castle that guarded the historic Mountain Pass between nicasia and corinia can be difficult to pick out from the White Stone Bluffs that protect [Music] it once you're on the site it's a real joy to explore the fragmented stairways walls and Towers while the view encapsulates a massive sweep of the northern coast St hyan started out as an Hermitage but the byzantin and then the lucidin Fortified the complex to defend against Arab Coastal rids at number five bayas Abbey one of the Island's most famous tourist attractions thanks to writer Lawrence derell bipas is a wonderfully old-fashioned Village of humble whitewashed Cottages that sprawl down the hillside ending at the evocative ruin of bipas Abbey below British author Lawrence Duro wrote the book bitter lemons of Cypress while living here immortalizing cypriate Village Life at the end of the British colonial period although the village is incredibly picturesque itself the main site is bipas Abbey an old augustinian Monastery full of intricately carved arches and Cypress trees surrounding its cloy at number four [Music] famagusta the old town of famagusta also called gazam magusa riddled with crumbling Gothic buildings is the most beautifully evocative town on the island ringed by a mighty border of well-preserved Phoenician fortifications the old town is stuffed full of Golden Stone ruins of basilicas and remnants of palaces that sit incongruously between rows of dilapidated houses right at the center is the Glorious laa Mustafa pasam mosque main Square which was once the Cathedral of AOS Nicolas and is a beautifully preserved example of Gothic architecture at number three in our list ruins of ancient salamis along with ancient Kuran ancient salamis is Cypress most important ancient Kingdom archaeological site with a wealth of ruins to explore the gymnasium and baths complex with its fine statuary and grandly columned Courtyard is the main attraction but further into the sprawling site are vast remnants of two basilicas an Agora and a huge Reservoir complex nearly all the remains date from the Ci's groman and Byzantine eras but salami's history stretches much further back at number two Bento Castle [Music] less visited than starian castle and more dilapidated buffavento Castle is a windy rugged Outpost of a ruin at top a craggy Mountain Summit to the east of corania the ruins here are not extensive but they lie in a picturesque location towering over the forested slopes below buffavento is thought to have been built during the Byzantine era and crusader Richard the lionart once took ownership of it strategically it played an important role in guarding the coastline along with st hyan Castle to the West check out the number one places in our list nicosia's Oldtown neighborhood although nicasia doesn't have a huge amount of big hitter tourist sites the true attraction of a visit here is simply strolling the narrow Lanes of the Oldtown District ledis street is the main drag through the old town and is lined with cafes restaurants and shops leading right up to the ledis street Green Line crossing post to North nicasia skirt off ledis Street into the tumble of Alleyways to check out the ottoman Mansion architecture of the House of hats of georgaki cornos 20 petriu Gregorio Street who was the Island's dragan during the late 18th century in the amarai mosque trupi street with which has a 14th century Luci signant entranceway now it's time to hear from you what's your favorite things to do in nicasia is there something we missed let us know in the comments section below and don't forget to give this video a like And subscribe to greenable if you haven't already clicked the Bell icon to stay updated on all our latest [Music] content [Music] n

videos.title: Top 25 Things To Do in Nicosia | Cyprus Travel Guide
videos.description:
Are you looking for the best things to do in Nicosia, the capital of Cyprus? Do you want to discover the rich history and culture of this divided city? Do you want to hike and explore all over the amazing Troodos Mountains? If you answered yes to any of these questions, then this video is for you!

In this video, I will show you the top 25 things to do in Nicosia, the ultimate Cyprus travel guide. You will see the most famous landmarks, such as the Venetian walls, Ledra Street, the Selimiye Mosque and the many museums of Nicosia. You will also explore some hidden gems, such as the old market, the buffer zone and a serene forest park located in the city. You will learn about the history of this fascinating city, which is split between the Greek Cypriot and Turkish Cypriot communities.

Timestamps: 
- Intro - 0:00
- The Old City - 0:29
- The Museums (Cyprus Museum, Leventis Gallery, & Folk Art Museum) - 0:56
- The Venetian Walls - 1:42
- Famagusta Gate (Nicosia Aqueduct & Liberty Monument) - 2:19
- Shacolas Tower Museum & Observatory - 2:52
- The House of Hadjigeorgakis Kornesios - 3:24
- Eleftheria Square - 3:53
- Ledra Street - 4:19
- North Nicosia - 4:42
- Büyük Han - 5:09
- Selimiye Camii - 5:23
- Nicosia Outskirts - 5:48
- Athalassa National Forest Park - 6:05
- The Mountains - 7:06
- Xyliatos Dam & Reservoir - 7:16
- Fikardou - 8:09
- Pikrovisi Tis Merikas Nature Trail - 8:47
- The Troodos Mountains (Troodos, Artemis Trail, Caledonia & Millomeris Waterfalls ) - 9:35
- Filani (Agia Marina) - 10:23
- Agios Sozomenos - 11:32
- Memi Mine Lake - 11:50
- The Giant Olive Tree - 12:32

videos.title: TOP 4 - Nicosia, Cyprus - Things to See and Do 🇨🇾
videos.description:
Welcome to another video!

In this episode we are going to Nicosia in Cyprus and I will show you the Top 7 Things to See and Do in Nicosia. 🌴🏰😎☀️

Nicosia is the capital of Cyprus and located in the middle of the island. It’s an ancient city which has been the capital of Cyprus since the 10th century. 

In 1567 Venetians built the new fortifications of Nicosia, which are well-preserved still to this day.

The capital and the island has been divided since the Turkish invasion in 1974 and it is now the worlds only divided capital. In the middle of the city you have the green line. In this video I will focus on the area south of the green line.

Nicosia has established itself as the island's financial capital and the main international business center.

Come along on this tour of the city and I will show you all of my favorite things in Nicosia. 

Please like and subscribe to the channel to see more content like this.

MY TOP 4 - THINGS TO DO AND SEE IN NICOSIA - CYPRUS

1. Shacolas Tower 
2. Eleftheria Square
3. Piatsa Gourounaki
4. Ledra Street

📌 Location: Limassol, Cyprus

✈️ Closest Airport: LCA - Larnaca Intl. Airport

💰 Budget: Hotels/B&B are around €80-140 per night. A meal is €8.00 - 18.00. A glass of wine is €6.00 - 10.00

Piatsa Gourounaki location:
Faneromenis 92 Nicosia CY 1011, Fanairomenis, Nicosia, Cyprus

Google Maps link: https://maps.app.goo.gl/WxSBGCkj9NbVR4rC7

Timecodes:

00:00 Intro
01:12 Shacolas Tower
02:13 Eleftheria Square
03:17  Piatsa Gourounaki
04:36 Ledra Street

videos.transcript: this is narrated travel Adventures I'm in the capital of Cypress nikosia and I'm here to show you the top things to do welcome to another video in this episode I will show you my top four things to see and do in [Music] nikosia nikosia is the capital of Cypress and located in the middle of the island it's an ancient city which has been the capital of Cypress since the 10th Century ad in 1567 venetians built the new fortifications of nikosia which are well preserved still to this day nikosia has established itself as the Island's Financial capital and the main International Business Center the Capitan island has been divided since the Turkish invasion in 1974 and it is now the world's only divided capital in the middle of the city you have the green line in this video I will focus on the area south of the green line number one on the list is the shaka's Tower museum and Observatory it's a good place to start exploring the city and to get a good overview of the layout take the elevator to the 11th floor and you will have a 360° view over the city the entrance is only €250 you can open the windows to get a clearer view to the north you can see the Turkish occupied parts of nikosia and Cypress there is also great information about the impressive history of nikosia [Music] to the South you see the Elia square and the high-rise buildings surround it number two on the list is the Elia Square the name literally meaning Liberty Square is the main Square in nikosia designed by renowned architect sahah Hadid the square blend's contemporary Aesthetics with its Rich historical context it serves as a vital public space promoting social interaction and urban connectivity the renovation integrates green areas pedestrian Pathways and modern amenities enhancing its appeal and functionality Elia Square symbolizes freedom and unity reflecting Cypress evolving identity opinions are divided about the Square's modern design some says it doesn't fit in within the context of nikosia being a historic City a lack of shade and trees is another negative [Music] opinion it's time to grab some good food and as number three on our list is Patza gurun Naki it's located on a small Street only a few minutes from shaka's Tower I will share the location in the description box they serve freshly made suvlaki grilled on charcoal really good quality and affordable prices this place was recommended by many we ordered a Greek salad and breed with tter to start tter is a spicy feta cheese dip [Music] then a chicken jetos in Cypress p and some chicken saki whoops let's go back as a free dessert they served us this panacota the bill came to €29 including drinks a highly recommended place with friendly staff number four on the list is lra Street it is the most famous street in nikosia and is lined with a mix of traditional shops modern boutiques cafes and [Music] restaurants it is now also the main Crossing Point through the green line on the 3rd of April 2008 at 9:00 a.m. local time the lra street roadblock Crossing through the UN buffer zone was reopened after 34 years in the presence of Greek and Turkish cypriot officials it's an historic day in Cyprus Greek and Turkish cypriots have torn down the barricades which have divided them for 44 years on a popular shopping street in the capital nikosia it has now been 50 years since the Turkish invasion of Cyprus and the island remains divided to this day [Music] thanks for watching please like And subscribe if you're new to the channel I have plenty of more videos from European cities and Beyond have a great day wherever you are bye now

videos.title: What's Going on in CYPRUS? | Travel Guide & Vlog in NICOSIA
videos.description:
Discover the beauty of Cyprus, an island filled with rich history, stunning beaches, and unique culture. From the ancient ruins of Kourion and Paphos to the vibrant city life in Nicosia, Cyprus offers a perfect mix of relaxation and adventure. In this guide, we’ll show you the must-see attractions, hidden gems, and essential travel tips to help you explore this Mediterranean paradise.

Whether you're lounging on the sandy shores of Fig Tree Bay, exploring historic sites, or indulging in delicious Cypriot cuisine, Cyprus is a must-visit destination. Watch this video for all the information you need to plan your unforgettable Cyprus trip. Don’t forget to like, comment, and subscribe for more travel guides!

#Cyprus #ExploreCyprus #CyprusTravel #Paphos #Nicosia #CypriotCuisine #MediterraneanTravel #VisitCyprus #TravelGuide

Tales From The Road & Tastes From The Road are a long time creative project of mine where I try to expose my viewers to new locations, food, cultures and peoples. I am a full time digital nomad, working to help people travel.

Project 197 | A new shorts and reels project where I am cooking a dish from every country in the world, including disputed regions and maybe more in between. Want to add a recipe to project to be featured on my channel ? Add a recipe in the link: https://forms.gle/je8QkA3Qoqbaqq1U7

Join me on Tales From The Road, your passport to unforgettable culinary and travel experiences. Embark on a captivating journey as we uncover hidden gems, savor delectable dishes, and immerse ourselves in vibrant cultures. From mouthwatering street food to luxurious gastronomic delights, our channel is your guide to wanderlust-inducing tales and tantalizing flavors from around the globe. Subscribe now to join our adventurous community and let us ignite your appetite for both food and travel!
